name: Krapf Group-Sch Bus & Trnsprtn
principals: Krapf family (founded by George Krapf, Jr.; six family members in management)
service_types: school bus, special needs, charter/motorcoach, public transit
districts_or_customers: Multiple districts across PA, DE, NJ; Wilmington DE terminal services local Delaware contracts
recent_news: Krapf Group remains one of the largest privately-held, family-owned school bus contractors in the U.S.; HQ in West Chester PA with the Wilmington DE entry being one operational terminal (~$250M total revenue 2026).
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Krapf is a top-10 national family-owned platform (~2,500 buses, $250M rev) and itself a likely consolidator/strategic competitor to Summit. The Wilmington DE listing is just one Krapf terminal, not a standalone target.

name: Shaw School Bus Lines Inc
principals: Patti Schooley (President); Susan J. Schooley (Owner per source data); the late James Patrick Schooley Jr. was co-owner
districts_or_customers: Western PA rural districts near Volant/New Castle (Wilmington Area SD area)
recent_news: USDOT 1166831 listed inactive in third-party carrier databases; family co-owner James P. Schooley Jr. obituary suggests generational transition underway.
fit_notes: Small, family-owned, rural western PA operator (~15 buses) with apparent succession signal (deceased co-owner, aging principal). Sub-scale alone but plausible bolt-on into a western-PA cluster; DOT inactive flag warrants diligence on operating status.

name: E-M Sch Trnsprtn Svc-Sw Fl
service_types: private/contracted school van transportation; charter school routes; parent-paid private service
districts_or_customers: Serves Collier County families; provides independent transport to Collier Charter Academy (NOT the official contractor — A&S Bus is) and other Naples-area schools
recent_news: Operates ~12 years per Buzzfile; operates as parent-contracted van service rather than district contractor; no M&A news found.
fit_notes: Marginal fit. Appears to be a small Hispanic-owned private van/parent-pay operator in Naples rather than a traditional district yellow-bus contractor. Limited strategic value to Summit unless used to seed a SW Florida footprint; revenue and fleet are small for a true tuck-in.

name: R & A School Bus Co Inc
districts_or_customers: Clearfield County PA area districts (Karthaus/Frenchville); likely West Branch Area SD and neighboring rural Clearfield districts
recent_news: PA-incorporated 5/7/1979; no M&A or sale news found; long-tenured leadership suggests possible succession opportunity.
fit_notes: Independent, family-owned rural central PA contractor (~$2.4M rev, ~20 buses) with 45+ year history and aging principals — classic succession profile. Sub-scale on its own; sensible as a bolt-on into a central PA cluster (would pair with Clearfield/Centre County operators).

name: John T Cyr & Sons Inc
principals: Joe Cyr (President); Michelle Ramsdell (Manager); Cyr family (founded by John T. Cyr 1912, son Joseph, grandson Harvey, Joe Cyr today)
service_types: school bus, motorcoach charter, intercity/scheduled bus (Cyr Bus Line), tours (Cyr Northstar Tours)
districts_or_customers: 13 school districts / 25 communities in Bangor & northern Maine (incl. Bangor, Brewer, Old Town); charter customers include University of Maine, Husson University, Colby College
recent_news: 112+ year family operator; HQ at 153 Gilman Falls Ave, Old Town ME (Detroit ME address per source data is likely a satellite yard). Long M&A track record as acquirer (Brewer service 1976, Bangor 1978, Bangor & Aroostook coaches 1984).
fit_notes: High-quality, true tuck-in target: ~250 school buses + 22 motorcoaches, dominant northern Maine market position, 13 district contracts, blue-chip charter customers. Multi-generational family ownership with potential succession dynamics. Would give Summit an instant Maine platform with limited geographic competitive overlap. Likely revenue is materially higher than the $1.8M in source data (which appears to reflect only the Detroit ME satellite).
